value.stringValue: Laboratory Support Technician / Junior Laboratory Assistant / General Operative

choices.name: Advanced
choices.choiceText: Advanced - I know how to use LIMS system and could lead and train others

choices.name: Intermediate
choices.choiceText: Intermediate - I would require a brief introduction and then would be comfortable using it

choices.name: Limited
choices.choiceText: Limited - I would require a complete refresh or assistance using it

fields.name: Completion_Text
fields.fieldText:
Submitted!

Thank you for taking the time to apply for a role with NHS Test and Trace, your application is now being reviewed by our recruitment team. 

If your application is successful, we’ll be in touch very soon to let you know about the next step in your application journey.


Due to the large amount of responses we are receiving for this role, it may not always be possible to give individual feedback so if you haven’t heard from us within three weeks, please assume your application has not been successful on this occasion.

We thank you for your interest in this position.


NHS Test and Trace

fields.name: Conflicts_of_Interest
fields.fieldText:
Declaring Conflict of Interest


In the public sector, a conflict of interest exists where a person's duties or responsibilities to a public entity could be affected by some other separate (and usually private) interest or duty that he or she may have. If you answer yes to this question, you will be asked to give details of any business or other interests or any personal connections which, if you are appointed, could be misconstrued or cause embarrassment to the NHS Test and Trace labs.


Any particular conflicts of interest detailed here will not prevent you going forward to interview and may not necessarily exclude you from employment but may, if appropriate, be explored with you during your interview to establish how you would address the issue(s) should you be successful in your application.


An example of a conflict of interest may include working within media.

fields.name: Conflict_of_Interest
fields.fieldText: If you are successful in this application, would there be any potential conflicts of interest?

fields.name: CV_Text
fields.fieldText:
CV Upload

Please upload your CV below:


Alert!

You will not be able to proceed without uploading a CV at this stage

fields.name: Data_Text
fields.fieldText:
Data Privacy Statement


Click Here to find information on the full privacy policy for the TT laboratory jobs website, an Impellam Group website (“Our Site”). The privacy notice sets out how to request an update to your marketing preferences and how Impellam Group entities Medacs Global Group Limited, Science Recruitment Group Limited and Blue Arrow Limited will manage personal data submitted via the TT laboratory jobs website and registration process.

fields.name: Privacy_Agreement
fields.fieldText: By selecting ”I agree” you confirm that you have read, understand and consent to the Data Privacy Statement

fields.name: Drop_Out_Text
fields.fieldText:
Thank you for taking time to apply to our advert, we appreciate the time you have taken to complete this application process. 


Unfortunately, on this occasion your application has been unsuccessful.


We wish you all the best with your job search and hope you find a position soon.


Kind regards and best wishes,


NHS Test and Trace

fields.name: High_Risk_Criteria_0
fields.fieldText:
Government criteria for high risk individuals


People at high risk from coronavirus include people who:

  • Have had an organ transplant
  • Are having chemotherapy or antibody treatment for cancer, including immunotherapy
  • Are having an intense course of radiotherapy for lung cancer
  • Are having targeted cancer treatments that can affect the immune system (such as protein kinase inhibitors or PARP inhibitors)
  • Have blood or bone marrow cancer (such as leukaemia, lymphoma or myeloma)
  • Have had a bone marrow or stem cell transplant in the past 6 months, or are still taking immunosuppressant medicine
  • Have been told by a doctor they have a severe lung condition (such as cycstic fibrosis, severe asthma or severe COPD)
  • Have a condition that means they have a very high risk of getting infections (such as SCID or sickle cell)
  • Are taking medication that makes them much more likely to get infections (such as high doses of steroids or immunosuppressant medicine)
  • Have a serious heart condition and are pregnant

fields.name: Confirm_Risk_Factors_0
fields.fieldText: Based on the high risk criteria, please confirm that you do not have any of the risk factors listed?

fields.name: Risk_Factor_Details_0
fields.fieldText: Please give further details:

fields.name: Shielding_Confirmation_0
fields.fieldText: Based on the high risk criteria, please confirm that you do not live with anyone who is shielding or has the risk factors listed?

fields.name: Shielding_Details_0
fields.fieldText: Please give further details:

fields.name: Job_Role_Text
fields.fieldText:
Job Role/Location Details


﻿We are adding new roles and locations on an ongoing basis, as new test centres are opened across the UK. If you don’t see a role or location which is suitable for you, please select ‘other’ to register your interest and join our talent pool.

fields.name: Job_Titles
fields.fieldText: Please select the job title(s) you’d like to apply for:

fields.name: Job_Role_Other
fields.fieldText: Please provide additional info about the “other” option:

fields.name: Location
fields.fieldText: Location

fields.name: Location_Other
fields.fieldText: Please let us know which location you're looking for:

fields.fieldText: Where did you learn about this job?

rightValue.stringValue: College careers team or school/college network

fields.fieldText: Have you been referred by the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) or a lab?

fields.fieldText: Have you been referred through a friend?

fields.fieldText: Please provide the referrer's First Name:

fields.fieldText: Are you looking for a full-time or part-time opportunity?

fields.fieldText: Which of the following shift patterns would you prefer?

fields.fieldText: Are you able to work weekends?

fields.fieldText: If needed, would you be able to work night shifts where necessary, or for an interim period of 6 months?

fields.fieldText: Have you ever worked in a lab before?

fields.fieldText: Mobile

validationRule.errorMessage: Email must be in a valid format

fields.fieldText: Are you between the age of 16 and 70?

fields.fieldText: Post Code

fields.name: Accessibility_Requirements
fields.fieldText: Do you have any accessibility requirements? Please note this will not affect your application

fields.name: Accessibility_Requirement_Details
fields.fieldText: Please provide further details - please note we may ask more around this on the telephone screen to ensure we can make any reasonable adjustments that may be required:

fields.name: Prescreen_Screen_Text
fields.fieldText: Pre-Screening Questions

fields.name: GCSE
fields.fieldText: Do you have a GCSE or equivalent in Maths and English, at grade C or above?

fields.name: GCSE_Science
fields.fieldText: Do you have a GCSE or equivalent in Science, at grade C or above?

fields.name: Right_to_Work
fields.fieldText: Do you have the right to work in the UK? (a valid UK passport and /or a valid visa)

fields.name: Interested_Lab_Work
fields.fieldText: Are you comfortable working in a laboratory environment?

value.stringValue: We just need to verify you are human before submitting these details

fields.name: Text_Question4
fields.fieldText: Qualifications

fields.name: Qualification_Choice
fields.fieldText: Please list the highest level qualification you currently hold:

fields.name: Other_Qualification
fields.fieldText: Please provide additional info about the “other” option:

fields.name: Qualification_Subject
fields.fieldText: Please confirm the subject of your highest qualification(s): e.g Molecular Biology

fields.name: Welcome_Text
fields.fieldText:
Application for National Testing Programme


As part of supporting efforts to reopen society and the economy, NHS Test and Trace is expanding our network of laboratories across the UK. This expansion has created an exceptional opportunity for both experienced scientists and those wanting to embark on a career within a laboratory environment. Our laboratories have access to the latest equipment and offer a truly unique and supportive environment.


We need a broad range of skillsets to support the expansion of the laboratories, from entry level positions for new graduates and those with good A levels to positions running our laboratories. We understand that in these current times our workforce requires flexibility and as such we can offer contracting, fixed term or permanent posts. The workforce will be deployed as part of the broader NHS Test and Trace programme.


The need to increase our testing capacity is immediate, so we can offer employment in a variety of roles nationwide, at very competitive pay rates with opportunities to broaden your experience and be part of a truly phenomenal initiative. 


Our laboratories will continue to expand over the coming months, accessing the very latest technology and scientific resources to support our community. This requires ongoing training and development which is delivered onsite by our experienced team members.


This is an exciting opportunity to work on the cutting edge of supporting the COVID-19 recovery efforts, to help reopen society and the economy. Our laboratory teams are critical to the success of the Programme. To register your interest in a full-time or part-time role at a COVID-19 laboratory, please begin your application now.


As part of the recruitment process, you may be subject to recruitment checks in line with UK government screening, a Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS) which comprises of verification for the following four main elements: Identity, Nationality and Immigration Status (including an entitlement to undertake the work in question), Employment History (past 3 years) and Criminal Record (unspent convictions only). Should you during this process supply any false information or fail to disclose relevant information it could be grounds for refusal/dismissal and could amount to a criminal offence.


Please note, although this portal is operated on behalf of NHS Test and Trace, employment opportunities are through third party suppliers such as Lighthouse Labs and specialist workforce providers
